title: Research Sprint: Find, Judge, and Test Web Guidance
summary: Turn an ambiguous defect question into a reproducible investigation with primary sources, current scope, browser evidence, and honest uncertainty.
objectives: Search from a precise behavior question rather than a vague symptom.
Produce a recommendation another developer can independently verify.

steps.title: Record uncertainty · research sprint
steps.instruction: In the standards-and-browser research sprint, two current sources disagree and the test is inconclusive. What is the professional response?
steps.why: Technical guidance is safer when authority, currency, scope, and browser evidence agree. Working through record uncertainty narrows the standards-and-browser research sprint while you defend the selected claim.

content.title: Record uncertainty — standards-and-browser research sprint
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the standards-and-browser research sprint evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During standards-and-browser research sprint answer, identify which source verification boundary “Record uncertainty” asks about.
Compare every standards-and-browser research sprint option with the named “Record uncertainty” evidence during answer.
For standards-and-browser research sprint answer, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Record uncertainty.”

options.text: Choose the shortest source and delete evidence of the disagreement.
Document the conflict, environment, and uncertainty; avoid presenting the decision as settled fact.
Present the preferred answer as universal because uncertainty reduces confidence.

steps.title: Rebuild devtools evidence · research sprint
steps.instruction: Within the standards-and-browser research sprint, reconstruct the devtools evidence in causal order.
steps.why: A deliberate panel choice turns browser inspection into evidence instead of random experimentation. Ordering the standards-and-browser research sprint exposes its first unsupported causal handoff.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the standards-and-browser research sprint.

hints: In the standards-and-browser research sprint sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate standards-and-browser research sprint devtools evidence events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the standards-and-browser research sprint sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: Reproduce the defect and state the expected behavior
Choose the panel that exposes the responsible layer
Run the smallest temporary experiment that tests the hypothesis
Repair saved source, reload, and verify the evidence again

steps.title: Separate source and DOM · research sprint
steps.instruction: In the standards-and-browser research sprint, which statement accounts for the recovered tree?
steps.why: Source, the browser-built DOM, layout, and pixels are related stages rather than interchangeable evidence. Working through separate source and dom narrows the standards-and-browser research sprint while you inspect the artifact.

content.title: Separate source and DOM — standards-and-browser research sprint
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the standards-and-browser research sprint evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During standards-and-browser research sprint inspect, identify which browser pipeline boundary “Separate source and DOM” asks about.
Compare every standards-and-browser research sprint option with the named “Separate source and DOM” evidence during inspect.
For standards-and-browser research sprint inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Separate source and DOM.”

options.text: Source and DOM must remain identical character for character after parsing.
Source is parser input; the DOM is the browser’s current constructed representation and may include recovery.
The DOM is a screenshot made from the final pixels.

stimulus.title: Recovered document tree · standards-and-browser research sprint
stimulus.caption: The browser-built structure after parsing malformed source. Case: standards-and-browser research sprint; focus: separate source and dom.

lines.label: parser
lines.text: end tags inferred before document end

steps.title: Identify the local journey · research sprint
steps.instruction: In the standards-and-browser research sprint, which evidence proves one load did not require notices.example?
steps.why: Local-file access and web requests can display similar pages but fail at different boundaries. Working through identify the local journey narrows the standards-and-browser research sprint before the result is shown.

content.title: Identify the local journey — standards-and-browser research sprint
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the standards-and-browser research sprint evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During standards-and-browser research sprint predict, identify which load journey boundary “Identify the local journey” asks about.
Compare every standards-and-browser research sprint option with the named “Identify the local journey” evidence during predict.
For standards-and-browser research sprint predict, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Identify the local journey.”

options.text: The HTTPS response includes a 200 status.
The file URL identifies a resource on local storage.
The remote host responded in 84 milliseconds.

steps.title: Diagnose the moved image · research sprint
steps.instruction: In the standards-and-browser research sprint, why does the current source reference fail on another learner’s computer?
steps.why: Portable paths preserve the relationship between project resources when the containing folder moves. Working through diagnose the moved image narrows the standards-and-browser research sprint as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: A project is a directory tree. A filename includes its real extension, and a relative path describes one resource from the current document location. Keeping related resources inside the project and testing after moving the whole folder exposes machine-specific absolute paths and case mismatches. Apply this distinction during the standards-and-browser research sprint read task.

hints: During standards-and-browser research sprint read, identify which project paths boundary “Diagnose the moved image” asks about.
Compare every standards-and-browser research sprint option with the named “Diagnose the moved image” evidence during read.
For standards-and-browser research sprint read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Diagnose the moved image.”

options.text: SVG files can only load from a desktop folder.
It names one user’s absolute machine location instead of the project-relative image location.
The browser requires every image to be copied into the HTML source text.

steps.title: Correct the mental model · research sprint
steps.instruction: In the standards-and-browser research sprint, which statement about HTML is defensible?
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Working through correct the mental model narrows the standards-and-browser research sprint while you inspect the artifact.

content.title: Correct the mental model — standards-and-browser research sprint
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the standards-and-browser research sprint evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During standards-and-browser research sprint inspect, identify which system chain boundary “Correct the mental model” asks about.
Compare every standards-and-browser research sprint option with the named “Correct the mental model” evidence during inspect.
For standards-and-browser research sprint inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Correct the mental model.”

options.text: HTML is executable machine code that runs without an application.
HTML is document data interpreted by a browser process running with operating-system and hardware support.
HTML is a physical display protocol sent directly to the monitor.

stimulus.title: System event trace · standards-and-browser research sprint
stimulus.caption: A simplified trace captured while a saved notice opens. Case: standards-and-browser research sprint; focus: correct the mental model.

steps.title: Rank the evidence · research sprint
steps.instruction: In the standards-and-browser research sprint, which combination provides the strongest basis for a platform claim?
steps.why: Technical guidance is safer when authority, currency, scope, and browser evidence agree. Working through rank the evidence narrows the standards-and-browser research sprint while you defend the selected claim.

content.title: Rank the evidence — standards-and-browser research sprint
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the standards-and-browser research sprint evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During standards-and-browser research sprint answer, identify which source verification boundary “Rank the evidence” asks about.
Compare every standards-and-browser research sprint option with the named “Rank the evidence” evidence during answer.
For standards-and-browser research sprint answer, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Rank the evidence.”

options.text: The first search snippet plus the number of advertisements on its page.
A current primary requirement, scoped official explanation, and reproducible browser test.
An undated forum answer repeated by several copied blog posts.

steps.title: Write the evidence note · research sprint
steps.instruction: Write a concise case note for the standards-and-browser research sprint. Explain the mechanism, cite observed evidence, and reject one plausible alternative.
steps.why: Explaining the standards-and-browser research sprint from memory exposes gaps that selected answers can hide. Cite the mechanism and its evidence.

content.title: Transfer brief
content.text: A teammate must be able to reproduce your conclusion without seeing the earlier answer choices. Name evidence and limits, not only the final verdict.

hints: Start the standards-and-browser research sprint case note with the earliest observed event.
Use these standards-and-browser research sprint technical anchors where they fit: primary source, version, browser evidence, Elements, Network.
Close the standards-and-browser research sprint note by naming one alternative and the evidence that rules it out.
